---
title: "Proof-Backed Showcase"
---

This page is the public-facing capability map for Circle Calculus. It is allowed to be inviting, but it is not allowed to float free of the proof corpus.

Every advertised item below is backed by `manifests/capability_showcase.yaml`. The validator `scripts/check_capability_showcase.py` requires each item to cite known papers, proved theorem ids, dictionary ids, source files, and pytest-discovered executable examples. The generated-data checker then recomputes the portfolio summary from those capability rows: every advertised theorem id must be carried by a cited paper, every advertised source ref must be backed by a cited paper Source Trail or cited Lean sidecar import closure, and every Living Book ref must carry the advertised page/widget evidence. If any theorem is unproved, unknown, not carried by the cited papers, not traceable to cited source evidence, or absent from its Living Book presentation, the checks fail.

::: {.warning-box}
This page advertises expressiveness and proof-linked interfaces. It does not upgrade executable examples, benchmark fixtures, imported theorem bridges, or roadmap ideas into new mathematical proofs.
:::

## Why This Page Exists

Circle Calculus should be judged by whether it can express serious structures in a way that is readable, executable, and formally auditable. The strongest current answer is not one slogan. It is a portfolio of checked handles:

- famous additive-combinatorics theorems stated in circle vocabulary,
- extremal and Ramsey theorem bridges with small finite examples,
- detailed modular period/fiber arithmetic,
- bounded quaternion and Hopf phase facts,
- finite gauge-path and holonomy interfaces,
- proof-carrying generated structures,
- cyclic AI schedule/index facts, and
- cyclic compute layout facts.

The generated matrix starts with four proof-backed reader routes: hard mathematics bridges, the cyclic proof spine, phase/finite-physics interfaces, and proof-carrying generated/AI structures. A route is ready only when every listed capability is known, fully claim-contracted, backed for its declared role, connected by an ordered proof trail, packaged as a skeptical-reader review packet, tied to a generated standard-parity/Circle-native-value comparison, bundled into a route-level reviewer dossier, summarized by a generated impact summary, and backed by the same theorem/source/Living Book evidence used below.

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="capability_portfolio_matrix"></div>

## Hard Mathematics Bridges

### Zero-Sum And Sumset Theorems

<div id="SHOW-001" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-001"></div>

Circle Calculus states EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport directly over `C_n` and `C_p`.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-paper-id="PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES" data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES.md">Zero-Sum Circles</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Classical zero-sum and additive-combinatorics theorems over cyclic groups, especially EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Finite circle types `C_n` and `C_p` present the cyclic groups directly, with theorem ids for zero-sum subsequences and prime-circle sumset growth.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The Circle framing makes zero-sum witnesses, sharpness families, and sumsets inspectable as finite address-space examples linked to Lean and Python sidecars.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can state established zero-sum and prime-circle sumset theorems directly in the native `C_n` vocabulary.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES/python/test_zero_sum_circle_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_01_ZERO_SUM_CIRCLES/python/test_zero_sum_circle_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></span><code>CC-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></span><code>CC-0201</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved Circle wrappers around mathlib's EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport theorems.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is a Circle wrapper over mathlib-formalized EGZ and Cauchy-Davenport, with Circle-specific examples and vocabulary layered on top.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No new proof of EGZ, no new proof of Cauchy-Davenport, and no progress claim on an open Erdos problem.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-theorem-id="CC-T0062"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-001" data-theorem-id="CC-T0063"></div>

### Katona And Erdos-Ko-Rado

<div id="SHOW-002" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-002"></div>

The Katona lane makes cyclic-order double counting and intersecting-family bounds visible while keeping the theorem source formal.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-paper-id="PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD" data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD.md">Katona and Erdos-Ko-Rado</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>The Katona circle method and the Erdos-Ko-Rado theorem for intersecting uniform set families.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Numberings, prefix-density counts, and intersecting families are exposed as theorem-backed finite-circle-method artifacts.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The project can show the circle-method proof atom as an executable finite count, then connect it to a formal EKR theorem card.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can expose a classical circle-method counting atom and the Erdos-Ko-Rado bound as proof-linked artifacts.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD/python/test_katona_ekr_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_02_KATONA_EKR_CIRCLE_METHOD/python/test_katona_ekr_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></span><code>CC-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></span><code>CC-0201</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Katona prefix-density and Erdos-Ko-Rado declarations.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is a Circle wrapper over mathlib-formalized Katona and Erdos-Ko-Rado declarations, plus finite Circle-method examples.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No new EKR proof and no classification of all extremal families.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-theorem-id="CC-T0064"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-002" data-theorem-id="CC-T0065"></div>

### Roth And Three-Term Progressions

<div id="SHOW-003" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-003"></div>

The Roth lane gives Circle Calculus a serious density-additive-combinatorics target with finite witness searches as examples.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-paper-id="PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES" data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES.md">Roth and Three-Term Progressions</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Roth's theorem and the asymptotic sublinearity of maximal three-term-AP-free subsets of natural intervals.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Dense finite interval examples and AP witness searches are connected to formal Roth theorem handles.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>Circle Math supplies a bridge toward cyclic AP models while keeping finite witness search, density claims, and Lean theorem status separated.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can connect finite witness searches to formal Roth-theorem handles.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES/python/test_roth_three_ap_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_03_ROTH_THREE_AP_CIRCLES/python/test_roth_three_ap_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></span><code>CC-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></span><code>CC-0201</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Roth theorem and Roth-number sublinearity statement.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is a Circle wrapper over mathlib-formalized Roth handles, with project finite-witness examples kept separate from the proof.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No new Roth proof, no improved bounds, and no cyclic-to-natural transfer theorem yet.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-theorem-id="CC-T0066"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-003" data-theorem-id="CC-T0067"></div>

### Ramsey Lines And Hales-Jewett

<div id="SHOW-004" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-004"></div>

The Ramsey lane shows unavoidable structure through formal Hales-Jewett and homothetic-copy handles.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-paper-id="PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES" data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES.md">Hales-Jewett and Ramsey Lines</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Hales-Jewett and Van der Waerden style unavoidable monochromatic-line or homothetic-copy theorems.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Finite coloring examples and combinatorial-line widgets can be attached to Lean theorem ids for the general Ramsey statements.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The Circle presentation can turn unavoidable structure into inspectable finite search artifacts without confusing examples for proofs.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can present Ramsey-style unavoidable structure with theorem ids and executable finite searches.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES/python/test_ramsey_hj_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_04_HALES_JEWETT_RAMSEY_CIRCLES/python/test_ramsey_hj_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></span><code>CC-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></span><code>CC-0201</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved wrappers around mathlib's Hales-Jewett and homothetic-copy theorems.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is a Circle wrapper over mathlib-formalized Ramsey/Hales-Jewett style theorems, with finite searches used only as examples.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>Finite Python searches are examples only; they are not the Hales-Jewett proof.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-theorem-id="CC-T0068"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-004" data-theorem-id="CC-T0069"></div>

### Circulant Graph Geometry

<div id="SHOW-005" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-005"></div>

The graph lane starts conservatively with cycle graphs, circulant graphs, connectedness, and unit-distance vocabulary.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-paper-id="PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS" data-paper-ref="papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/erdos/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS.md">Unit-Distance Circulant Graphs</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Standard cycle graphs, circulant graphs, graph connectedness, and unit-distance embedding vocabulary.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>The one-step finite circle graph is presented as a circulant graph, with connectedness and a minimal unit-distance embedding constructor.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The graph lab makes cyclic graph structure explicit before moving to harder Euclidean incidence questions.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can use cyclic graph models as a proof-backed bridge toward graph-geometry language.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS/python/test_circulant_graph_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_ERDOS_05_UNIT_DISTANCE_CIRCULANT_GRAPHS/python/test_circulant_graph_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></span><code>CC-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></span><code>CC-0201</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/erdos_bridges.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved cycle/circulant and connectedness bridges plus a unit-distance embedding constructor for empty graphs.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for cyclic graph structures and a minimal unit-distance embedding constructor, aligned with standard graph vocabulary.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No progress on hard unit-distance or distinct-distance problems.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-theorem-id="CC-T0070"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-theorem-id="CC-T0071"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-005" data-theorem-id="CC-T0072"></div>

## Circle-Native Strengths

### Periods, Fibers, Kernels, And Normal Forms

<div id="SHOW-006" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-006"></div>

The S1 spine is the internal proof engine: period, gcd orbit count, scaling images, fibers, kernel representatives, and full-coil conditions.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-paper-id="PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES" data-paper-ref="papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Finite Circles</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-paper-id="PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS" data-paper-ref="papers/S1/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S1/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS.md">Factors, Scaling, Prime Coils</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Standard modular arithmetic, cyclic group orbits, gcd periods, kernels, images, and fibers of multiplication maps.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>The S1 theorem spine expresses these facts as finite-circle period, orbit, scaling, image, kernel, and fiber theorems.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>Circle Math organizes modular facts around movement, closure, fibers, and representatives, making them easier to browse and test.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can express detailed modular-arithmetic structure as period, orbit, kernel, and fiber theorems.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS/python/test_paper_s1_04_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_S1_04_FACTORS_SCALING_PRIME_COILS/python/test_paper_s1_04_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-dictionary-id="S1-0001"></span><code>S1-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-dictionary-id="CC-0105"></span><code>CC-0105</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-dictionary-id="CC-0205"></span><code>CC-0205</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-dictionary-id="CC-0207"></span><code>CC-0207</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0041"></span><code>COMMON-0041</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-page-ref="site/chapters/S1/03_coils_orbits_closure.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/S1/03_coils_orbits_closure.qmd</code>; <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-page-ref="site/chapters/S1/04_period_gcd_prime_full_coils.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/S1/04_period_gcd_prime_full_coils.qmd</code>; widgets <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-widget-id="coil_orbit_explorer"></span><code>coil_orbit_explorer</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-widget-id="period_gcd_visualizer"></span><code>period_gcd_visualizer</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-widget-id="prime_full_coil_explorer"></span><code>prime_full_coil_explorer</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved S1 scaling and orbit spine, including full-coil iff coprime and image-times-fiber factorizations.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code in the S1 finite-circle spine, backed by paper and Python sidecar examples.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No claim that this replaces standard modular arithmetic; it is a proof-carrying cyclic presentation.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0006"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0048"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0049"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0054"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-006" data-theorem-id="CC-T0061"></div>

### Quaternion And Hopf Phase Coordinates

<div id="SHOW-007" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-007"></div>

The S3 lane gives bounded phase-coordinate, quaternion, pure-vector, and spin-sign facts that are useful for rotation, hidden phase, and sign-ambiguity explanations.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-paper-id="PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS" data-paper-ref="papers/S3/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S3/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS.md">Quaternion Coils</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-paper-id="PAPER_S3_03_HOPF_COILS" data-paper-ref="papers/S3/PAPER_S3_03_HOPF_COILS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S3/PAPER_S3_03_HOPF_COILS.md">Hopf Coils</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-paper-id="PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP" data-paper-ref="papers/S3/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S3/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP.md">Spin Double Cover Roadmap</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Quaternion unit algebra, Hopf-style phase coordinates, conjugation, and hidden phase symmetries.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Bounded quaternion, spin-sign, pure-vector, and Hopf coordinate facts are exposed through finite theorem spines with explicit topology warnings.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The Circle framing separates visible base coordinates from hidden phase in a way that is useful for teaching, widgets, and proof audits.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can express bounded quaternion, spin-sign, pure-vector, and Hopf phase facts with explicit warnings about topology boundaries.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS/python/test_quaternion_coil_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS/python/test_quaternion_coil_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_S3_03_HOPF_COILS/python/test_paper_s3_03_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_S3_03_HOPF_COILS/python/test_paper_s3_03_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP/python/test_spin_conjugation_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP/python/test_spin_conjugation_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3Q-0001"></span><code>S3Q-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3H-0001"></span><code>S3H-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3H-0002"></span><code>S3H-0002</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3S-0001"></span><code>S3S-0001</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3S-0002"></span><code>S3S-0002</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-dictionary-id="S3S-0003"></span><code>S3S-0003</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-page-ref="site/chapters/S3/hopf_coils.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/S3/hopf_coils.qmd</code>; <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/physics.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/physics.qmd</code>; widgets <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-widget-id="hopf_hidden_phase"></span><code>hopf_hidden_phase</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-widget-id="spin_sign_ambiguity"></span><code>spin_sign_ambiguity</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved quaternion unit laws, pure-vector conjugation boundary, sign-cancellation facts, and bounded Hopf phase-action facts.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for bounded quaternion, spin-sign, pure-vector, and Hopf-coordinate facts, with topology limits stated explicitly.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No full smooth Hopf fibration formalization and no complete SO(3) robotics formalization.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3Q-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3Q-T0008"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3H-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3H-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3H-T0008"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3S-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-007" data-theorem-id="S3S-T0005"></div>

## Application Interfaces

### Finite Gauge Paths

<div id="SHOW-008" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-008"></div>

The physics lane builds proof-carrying finite gauge paths with checked path composition and holonomy interfaces.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-paper-id="PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE" data-paper-ref="papers/physics/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/physics/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE.md">Proof-Carrying Lattice Gauge</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-portfolio-role="application_guardrail"></span><code>application_guardrail</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Discrete gauge-link paths, holonomy products, Wilson-loop style invariants, and checked path concatenation.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Finite gauge links and paths are represented with Lean-checked source-target and holonomy laws.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>Circle Math contributes proof-carrying finite records that can guard simulations and widgets against invalid path composition.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can build a finite proof-carrying gauge-path model with endpoint and holonomy laws.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE/python/test_lattice_gauge_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_PHYS_01_PROOF_CARRYING_LATTICE_GAUGE/python/test_lattice_gauge_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0060"></span><code>COMMON-0060</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0061"></span><code>COMMON-0061</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0062"></span><code>COMMON-0062</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0063"></span><code>COMMON-0063</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/physics.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/physics.qmd</code>; widgets <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-widget-id="finite_path_algebra"></span><code>finite_path_algebra</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-widget-id="finite_gauge_loop_holonomy"></span><code>finite_gauge_loop_holonomy</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-widget-id="wilson_loop_certificate"></span><code>wilson_loop_certificate</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved finite link-path, checked-path, path-plus-reverse zero-holonomy, gauge-transform, Wilson-loop, plaquette, closed-loop record, gauge-shifted closed-loop, two-path cycle gauge-invariance, two-path basepoint-swap facts, and one-step/two-step three-path cycle basepoint-rotation facts.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for finite gauge-link/path records and holonomy laws, with Python fixtures serving as executable references.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No continuum gauge theory, no physics discovery, and no lattice-QCD correctness claim.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0012"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0026"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0038"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0042"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0046"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0047"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0049"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0050"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0051"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0052"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0053"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0054"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0055"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0056"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0057"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0058"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0059"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0060"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-008" data-theorem-id="PHYS-T0061"></div>

### Generative Provenance

<div id="SHOW-009" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-009"></div>

The generative lane pairs seed-rule construction with exact-regeneration checks and negative-case gates.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-paper-id="PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE" data-paper-ref="papers/generative/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/generative/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE.md">Seed-Rule Provenance</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-paper-id="PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS" data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS.md">Proof-Carrying Glyphs</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-portfolio-role="application_guardrail"></span><code>application_guardrail</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Formal provenance, exact regeneration checks, orbit decompositions, and proof-carrying artifact records.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Seed rules, generator comparisons, proof glyphs, and exactness gates are expressed as finite Circle records and Lean facts.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>This is a Circle-native value proposition: generated artifacts can carry exact source trails and negative-case checks instead of loose visual claims.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can pair generated structures with checked provenance and exact-comparison gates.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE/python/test_seed_rule_provenance_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_GEN_01_SEED_RULE_PROVENANCE/python/test_seed_rule_provenance_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS/python/test_proof_glyph_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_P2_05_PROOF_CARRYING_GLYPHS/python/test_proof_glyph_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0033"></span><code>COMMON-0033</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0064"></span><code>COMMON-0064</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0065"></span><code>COMMON-0065</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0066"></span><code>COMMON-0066</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/generative.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/generative.qmd</code>; widgets <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-widget-id="seed_rule_diagram_generator"></span><code>seed_rule_diagram_generator</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-widget-id="orbit_family_generator"></span><code>orbit_family_generator</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-widget-id="proof_glyph_certificate"></span><code>proof_glyph_certificate</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-widget-id="generator_comparison_search"></span><code>generator_comparison_search</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved seed-rule, finite-circle node membership/no-duplicate/nonempty, orbit-decomposition, proof-glyph, generated-list length, exact-regeneration, negative-case, empty declared-search boundary, singleton exact-search, best-candidate soundness, exact-candidate absence/count gates, exact-candidate list soundness, and singleton membership gates.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for seed-rule provenance, proof glyphs, exact-regeneration gates, and negative-case search boundaries.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No universal compression claim and no guarantee that generated artifacts are mathematically interesting.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0008"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0017"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0019"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0020"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0021"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0022"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0024"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0026"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0027"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0028"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0029"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0030"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0031"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0032"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0033"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0034"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0035"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0036"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0037"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0038"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0039"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0040"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0041"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0042"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="GEN-T0043"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-009" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0006"></div>

### AI Schedules And Index Safety

<div id="SHOW-010" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-010"></div>

The AI lane proves finite schedule/index facts that can support experiments without pretending proofs imply model quality.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-paper-id="PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY" data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Coil Attention And Memory</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-paper-id="PAPER_AI_03_COILRA_AND_MULTICOIL_ROPE" data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_03_COILRA_AND_MULTICOIL_ROPE.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_03_COILRA_AND_MULTICOIL_ROPE.md">CoilRA And MultiCoil RoPE</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-portfolio-role="application_guardrail"></span><code>application_guardrail</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Finite cyclic buffers, schedule normalization, recurrence budgets, and modular adapter indexing.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>AI-facing memory slots, loop budgets, exit certificates, looped recurrent states, middle-block routes, combined route/budget schedules, and adapter blocks are encoded as finite cyclic address/schedule theorems.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>Circle Math can make experimental cyclic AI components auditable before any model-quality or speed claim is made.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can prove finite indexing and schedule-safety facts that AI experiments can reuse.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/test_memory_slot_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY/python/test_memory_slot_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_AI_03_COILRA_AND_MULTICOIL_ROPE/python/test_adapter_block_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_AI_03_COILRA_AND_MULTICOIL_ROPE/python/test_adapter_block_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0028"></span><code>COMMON-0028</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0047"></span><code>COMMON-0047</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0052"></span><code>COMMON-0052</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0067"></span><code>COMMON-0067</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0068"></span><code>COMMON-0068</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0071"></span><code>COMMON-0071</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/ai.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/ai.qmd</code>; widgets <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="cyclic_memory_slots"></span><code>cyclic_memory_slots</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="loop_recurrence_budget"></span><code>loop_recurrence_budget</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="loop_exit_certificate"></span><code>loop_exit_certificate</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="token_level_recurrence"></span><code>token_level_recurrence</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="learned_middle_block_recurrence"></span><code>learned_middle_block_recurrence</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="training_free_loop_wrapper"></span><code>training_free_loop_wrapper</code>, <span class="living-book-widget-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-widget-id="adapter_parameter_budget"></span><code>adapter_parameter_budget</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved finite memory-slot, loop-budget, active-token-step, looped recurrent-state, positive raw-budget closure, middle-block route, combined route/budget one-cycle and multi-cycle common-cycle closure, recurrence-budget positivity, unavailable-budget clamp, loop-exit, certificate-availability, certificate-budget-selection, certificate multi-pass invariance, and adapter-block normalization facts.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for finite cyclic memory, loop-budget, looped recurrent-state, positive raw-budget closure, active-step, middle-block route, combined route/budget common-cycle closure, certificate, and adapter-index safety facts; benchmarks remain separate.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No model-quality, speed, context-length, or reasoning improvement claim without benchmark evidence.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0011"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0017"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0022"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0024"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0025"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0031"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0032"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0033"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0034"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0035"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0036"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0037"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0038"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0039"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0040"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0041"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0042"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0043"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0044"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0045"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0046"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0047"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0048"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0056"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0049"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0050"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0051"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0052"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0053"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0054"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0055"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-010" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0005"></div>

### Circular Compute Layouts

<div id="SHOW-011" class="capability-anchor" data-capability-id="SHOW-011"></div>

The compute lane proves address-safety and schedule-normalization facts before any backend performance claim is allowed.

<p data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-paper-id="PAPER_COMP_01_PROOF_CARRYING_CIRCULAR_COMPUTATION" data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_01_PROOF_CARRYING_CIRCULAR_COMPUTATION.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_01_PROOF_CARRYING_CIRCULAR_COMPUTATION.md">Circular Computation</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-paper-id="PAPER_COMP_03_COIL_LAYOUT_STENCIL_NTT" data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_03_COIL_LAYOUT_STENCIL_NTT.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_03_COIL_LAYOUT_STENCIL_NTT.md">Coil Layout Stencil NTT</a></p>
<p data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-paper-id="PAPER_COMP_04_COIL_SYSTEMS_APPLICATIONS" data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_04_COIL_SYSTEMS_APPLICATIONS.md">Paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_04_COIL_SYSTEMS_APPLICATIONS.md">Coil Systems Applications</a></p>
- Portfolio roles: <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-portfolio-role="standard_math_parity"></span><code>standard_math_parity</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-portfolio-role="circle_native_value"></span><code>circle_native_value</code>; <span class="role-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-portfolio-role="application_guardrail"></span><code>application_guardrail</code>.
- Standard anchor: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="standard_math_anchor"></span>Circular buffers, periodic stencils, stride-address formulas, and round-robin scheduling laws.
- Circle expression: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="circle_math_expression"></span>Compute layouts are expressed as finite address normalization, stride-address, and schedule-preservation theorems.
- Circle-native value: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="circle_native_value"></span>The Circle form can certify when cyclic structure is real enough to justify backend experiments, while requiring benchmarks for performance claims.
- Advertised claim: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="advertised_claim"></span>Circle Calculus can prove finite address-safety and schedule-normalization facts for cyclic computation.
- Executable evidence: <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_COMP_01_PROOF_CARRYING_CIRCULAR_COMPUTATION/python/test_cyclic_address_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_COMP_01_PROOF_CARRYING_CIRCULAR_COMPUTATION/python/test_cyclic_address_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_COMP_03_COIL_LAYOUT_STENCIL_NTT/python/test_stride_address_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_COMP_03_COIL_LAYOUT_STENCIL_NTT/python/test_stride_address_examples.py</code>; <span class="executable-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-executable-ref="sidecars/PAPER_COMP_04_COIL_SYSTEMS_APPLICATIONS/python/test_round_robin_slot_examples.py"></span><code>sidecars/PAPER_COMP_04_COIL_SYSTEMS_APPLICATIONS/python/test_round_robin_slot_examples.py</code>.
- Vocabulary evidence: <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0018"></span><code>COMMON-0018</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0022"></span><code>COMMON-0022</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0040"></span><code>COMMON-0040</code>; <span class="dictionary-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0045"></span><code>COMMON-0045</code>.
- Living Book evidence: <span class="living-book-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-page-ref="site/chapters/applications/compute.qmd"></span><code>site/chapters/applications/compute.qmd</code>.
- Scope: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="proof_scope"></span>Lean-proved cyclic-address, stride-address, and round-robin schedule laws with Python layout fixtures.
- Proof provenance: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="proof_provenance"></span>Proof provenance is project-native Lean code for cyclic-address, stride-layout, and round-robin schedule laws, with performance claims left to explicit benchmarks.
- Boundary: <span class="claim-field-evidence" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-claim-field="not_claimed"></span>No performance claim without direct dense, FFT/NTT, and backend baselines.

<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-theorem-id="COMPC-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-theorem-id="COMPL-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-capability-id="SHOW-011" data-theorem-id="COMPS-T0004"></div>

## Verification Contract

The showcase source is:

```text
manifests/capability_showcase.yaml
```

The validator is:

```text
python scripts/check_capability_showcase.py
```

That check is part of `make sourcecheck`, so the advertised surface is kept under the same proof-status discipline as the rest of the project.

<script type="module" src="widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="widgets/showcase/capability_portfolio_matrix.js"></script>
